rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical [3+2] cycloaddition between N-benzyl-N,N-bis(trimethylsilylmethyl)amine precursors and electron-poor alkenes to form 3- and 3,4-disubstituted pyrrolidines via in situ anodic generation of a nonstabilized azomethine ylide.
electrochemical_role: Anodic single-electron oxidation of the bis(silylmethyl)amine 1a to its aminium radical cation, followed by a second SET after desilylation to give an iminium cation; cathode reduces water to H2 and hydroxide, which traps the TMS+ cation as silanol.
electron_flow: At the anode, amine 1a undergoes SET to give aminium radical cation A; loss of TMS+ yields neutral α-amino radical that is further oxidized (second SET) to iminium cation B; a second desilylation generates the nonstabilized azomethine ylide C, which undergoes concerted [3+2] cycloaddition with the electron-poor alkene 2a to give pyrrolidine 3aa. At the cathode, water is reduced to H2 and HO–, which quenches TMS+ to silanol.
rate_determining_step: Initial anodic SET oxidation of the amine 1a (E_ox = 0.75 V vs Ag/AgCl), as 2a shows no oxidation peak.

probe_reagents.name: Cyclic voltammetry of 1a, 2a, and 1a+2a
probe_reagents.observation: 1a oxidizes at 0.75 V vs Ag/AgCl; 2a shows no oxidation peak, supporting selective anodic oxidation of the amine precursor.

notes: Mechanism is proposed based on CV studies and literature precedent; no radical traps, KIE, or DFT reported. The TMS+ byproduct is quenched by cathodically generated hydroxide to give trimethylsilanol, consistent with a paired-electrolysis scenario. Gram-scale synthesis (38 h, 70%) supports robustness but is not mechanistic evidence.
